title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
During a confrontation, Micky reveals to Calgary and Mike that he, Mary, Tina, Hester, and Jacques are all adopted children. Micky explains that their mother built a family through adoption because she could not have biological children, but family dynamics soured when she chose Jacques as her favorite. Micky expresses resentment toward Jacques, refusing to acknowledge him as a brother, and argues that proving Jacques's innocence has not brought relief to the family, but rather new complications.

Later, Dr. Arthur Calgary meets with a lawyer named Mr. Marshall to discuss the consequences of his actions. Calgary explains that his attempt to clear Jack Ackerley's name has not provided closure but has instead opened a "new chapter" of worry and fear for the family. Calgary feels a sense of responsibility for the situation, noting that his involvement in the case began nearly two years ago when he gave a young man a ride.

Marshall explains the family's unease: because the murder occurred in a locked house with the blinds down, the killer must have been someone known to the deceased. Marshall suggests the family would have preferred Jack Ackerley to remain the convicted murderer because his reputation as a delinquent provided a simple, albeit tragic, resolution. Now that Jack has received a posthumous pardon, the case will be retried. Marshall expresses skepticism that the police will find new concrete evidence, though he suspects they may already have a hunch about the true killer. Calgary concludes the conversation by mentioning that Hester Ager said something regarding the situation.
